chapters.notes: Opening, grace-gifts, no lack, divisions, Chloe report, faction names, baptism/name issue, cross as foolishness/power, Divine wisdom overturning status-wisdom, no flesh boasting, Messiah as wisdom/righteousness/sanctification/release.

Paul's weakness/trembling, proclamation not in performance-wisdom, Spirit and power, hidden Divine wisdom, Spirit searching depths of the Divine, natural/soul-level versus spiritual discernment, mind of Messiah.

Fleshly-patterned factionalism, milk/solid food, Paul/Apollos as servants, planting/watering/God causing growth, God's field/building, foundation Jesus Messiah, work tested by fire, plural community as temple, no boasting in humans.

Attendants of Messiah and stewards of Divine mysteries, trustworthiness, no premature judgment, hidden counsels of hearts, not beyond what is written, everything received, apostolic humility, fatherly admonition, Timothy, reign of Divine in power not word.
Severe sexual immorality case, communal arrogance instead of mourning, discipline as communal protection and restoration, leaven/Passover imagery, sincerity and truth, judging inside not outside.

Lawsuits among siblings, communal defeat, injustice vice list with anti-weaponization note, washed/sanctified/made just, freedom not mastery by appetite, body for Lord, bodies as Messiah members and temple of holy Spirit.
Marriage and singleness counsel, mutual conjugal authority, concession not command, mixed marriage and peace, call/condition, present distress, freedom from human enslavement, undistracted devotion.
Food offered to idols, knowledge puffs but love builds, one Divine and one Lord, conscience as tender communal reality, liberty restrained by love, wounding sibling conscience as sin against Messiah.

translation_notes: 1 Corinthians should preserve Paul's pastoral correction of a gifted but fractured assembly without flattening it into shame or control.
Sophia should be wisdom; moria foolishness; dynamis power; pneuma Spirit/breath; psychikos natural/soul-level according to context; sarkikos fleshly-patterned according to context.
Ekklesia should remain assembly/community, not automatically institutional church.
Schisma should be split/division; keep the body-healing frame visible.
Charisma should be grace-gift/gift; charis remains grace.
Agape should be love, especially chapter 13 as the governing center for gifts and knowledge.
Porneia should be sexual immorality/misuse according to context, with care not to shame bodies or sexuality as such.
Soma/body should be treated positively; the body is for the Lord and becomes temple/embodied belonging.
Do not make Paul anti-body, anti-woman, or anti-Jewish. Preserve context, rhetoric, and pastoral purpose.
Mystical companion layer remains interpretive and does not replace source-audited restored text.
Verse-number alignment must remain intact in both restored and mystical companion files.

1 Corinthians 1-4 establishes the opening diagnosis: a gifted assembly fractured by faction, status-wisdom, and spiritual ego. Paul's medicine is the cross as Divine wisdom/power, Spirit-depth perception, God's field/building/temple imagery, no boasting in humans, and trustworthy stewardship of mysteries.

1 Corinthians 5-8 applies body/community medicine: discipline as protection not shame, body-positive holiness, freedom not mastery by appetite, marriage/singleness counsel under present distress, conscience-sensitive love, and knowledge governed by love.
next_actions: Continue 1 Corinthians 9-12: Paul's apostolic freedom and self-limitation, race/discipline imagery, Israel wilderness warnings, table fellowship and idols, head covering/order, Lord's supper abuses, spiritual gifts, one body many members.

Add glossary entries or reuse hooks: sophia, moria, dynamis, pneuma, psychikos, sarkikos, ekklesia, schisma, charisma, agape, porneia, soma, temple/body, conscience, liberty, and oikonomia/stewardship.
